title: Why build an alert rule query?

blocks.content:
The query is the foundation of every alert rule. It defines exactly what data Grafana watches — a metric like CPU usage, an error rate, or a count of log lines.

A well-built query is the difference between an alert that catches real problems and one that stays silent or fires on noise. Before you set a threshold or route a notification, you need a query that returns the data worth monitoring.

Getting the query right early pays off later: the condition you set, the labels you add, and the notifications you send all build on top of it.

In the next milestone, you learn how to open the alert rule form and name your rule.
